prompts: A genetic mutation occurs when there's an alteration in the DNA sequence, specifically involving changes in nucleotides. DNA is composed of nucleotides, which include adenine (A), thymine (T), cytosine (C), and guanine (G). Mutations can involve substitution, insertion, or deletion of one or more nucleotides. A substitution replaces one nucleotide with another, while insertions and deletions add or remove nucleotides, respectively. These changes can occur due to errors during DNA replication, exposure to mutagens, or environmental factors, potentially affecting gene function and leading to various biological consequences.
